description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a yellowish-tan body and two sets of straight, rectangular wings stacked vertically. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which features a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer with squared tips. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the cockpit is enclosed with a dark canopy situated just behind the propeller. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, all fixed and exposed. The surface of the airplane is marked with visible panel lines, rivets, and some weathering, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance.
description: A small, yellowish biplane with stacked wings, a single propeller, and an enclosed cockpit. The body shows panel lines, rivets, and a weathered finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked biplane wings, tail number, and the single propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual wings, propeller, and fixed landing gear beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the cockpit canopy, wing struts, and tail details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the alignment of the biplane wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, rectangular wings stacked vertically; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a dark canopy just behind the propeller.
